content: Vite PWA on GitHub Pages requires base:'/<REPO>/' in vite.config.ts; vite-plugin-pwa reads this automatically for SW scope and manifest start_url. Key pitfalls: (1) forgetting base causes all asset paths to be root-relative, breaking everything on /little-words/; (2) SW scope defaults to base path — if scope is set manually and conflicts, browser throws 'Failed to update a ServiceWorker for scope'; (3) manifest icons use relative paths which must account for base in icon src; (4) hash routing (#/route) is required for GitHub Pages — history API pushState will 404 on refresh because no server rewrite rule exists. Correct pattern: set base in vite.config.ts, let vite-plugin-pwa inherit it, use createHashRouter in React.
